element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Gating mechanism for world progression; 4 cards to access Birdie's area
Novel platforming obstacle; reverses gravity and player orientation
Spatial navigation tool; shortcuts within levels
Dual-purpose vehicle; traversal and transport element
Rocket acquisition; gateway to moon area
Gating progression to volcano area; moon environment introduces space theme
Final collection requirement before magic bag boss
Final boss; attacks with flag, magic wand, rabbit-in-hat projectiles
Hidden infinite lives function (bugged in retail version)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation with mechanical novelty as pacing device; each world introduces new mechanics to maintain engagement; puzzle card requirements provide gating for breathing room between intensity peaks

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Spinner blocks and zipper entrances serve as spatial discovery cues; hidden puzzle cards encourage exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Ronald's PuzzLeWoRlD contains hidden infinite lives function accessible by collecting all Arches in all three stages (mechanic is buggy in final release)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: Ronald's Clubhouse teaches basic jump, throw, collect mechanics with 4 puzzle cards as primary goal
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive complexity: Spinner blocks and zipper mechanics introduced; Birdie (5 cards), Grimace (3 cards), and Professor (5 cards) areas escalate spatial puzzle requirements
difficulty_curve.late_game: High challenge: Moon area (CosMc, 5 cards) and volcano (Hamburglar, 6 cards) present dangerous environments; final Magic Bag boss requires pattern recognition and projectile avoidance
notable_spikes: Introduction of Spinner gravity-reversal mechanic (moderate spike)
Magic Bag boss multi-phase attack pattern (flag, wand, rabbit projectiles)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each world requires collection of mandatory puzzle cards before progression. World 1: 4 cards → World 2: 5 cards → World 3: 3 cards → World 4: 5 cards → World 5: 5 cards → World 6: 6 cards. Creates rhythmic checkpoint structure and forces thorough exploration.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ronald's Clubhouse (4 cards unlock Birdie progression)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each McDonald's character (Ronald, Birdie, Grimace, Professor, CosMc) serves as NPC hub that gates world access and narratively justifies progression. Creates story-paced learning curve.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Professor's Workshop (introduces rocket vehicle as quest item)

notable_level_patterns.description: Progression moves from grounded McDonaldland (Clubhouse, Treehouse, Highlands) → abstract (Moon) → elemental danger (Volcano) → final showdown (Magic Bag confrontation). Spatial design mirrors narrative escalation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World 5 Moon area contrasts with earlier pastoral/domestic environments

notable_level_patterns.description: Spinner blocks (gravity inversion) and Zipper entrances (warp shortcuts) are embedded as level design vocabulary rather than enemy encounters. Encourages spatial problem-solving alongside combat.

sources_used: tcrf - M.C. Kids (The Cutting Room Floor)
